concerns.text: FR-003 requires LLM consensus validation on a random sample of flagged calls. Tasks T014 (marked FAILED), T013b (pending), and T013c (pending) are missing or broken. No active task covers the implementation of the sampling logic, sample size calculation, or the consensus validation loop itself, leaving FR-003 unimplemented.

US-1 and SC-001 require measuring the NDCG@10 drop against a 'unique subset' baseline. Tasks T015a (generate unique subset) and T015b (run baseline on unique subset and calculate drop) are marked pending with no implementation. Without these, the core hypothesis of US-1 cannot be tested.

FR-006 and Constitution Principle VII require hard enforcement of 6h/7GB limits. While T004a covers the watchdog, Task T008 ('Setup environment validation script to verify CPU-only constraints') is marked pending/rejected. The plan explicitly requires verifying CPU-only constraints; the absence of a task to implement this validation script leaves a gap in the resource constraint verification chain.

Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility) and Principle V (Versioning) require documentation and clean code. Tasks T032 (Documentation) and T033 (Code cleanup) are marked pending/rejected. Without these, the project cannot satisfy the reproducibility requirements of the constitution.

Task T014 is marked 'FAILED: unspecified' in the list. This is a stale state from a previous iteration that blocks the requirement. It needs to be either resolved (re-implemented) or replaced by a new task ID that covers the LLM consensus logic, rather than leaving a 'FAILED' marker in the active task list.
